text.title: Gce Managed Instance Groups Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gce-managed-instance-groups methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Single-zone outage takes the whole MIG offline.
antipattern.root-cause: MIG created as zonal, not regional.
antipattern.fix: Recreate as regional MIG distributing across ≥3 zones.

antipattern.symptom: Unhealthy instances stay in rotation; users hit failures.
antipattern.root-cause: MIG has no auto-healing policy or health check.
antipattern.fix: Add HTTP health check + auto-healing policy with initial-delay ≥ boot time.

antipattern.symptom: Too many instances replaced at once; capacity dips.
antipattern.root-cause: `updatePolicy` not set OR maxUnavailable too high.

antipattern.symptom: Manual edits disappear on auto-heal; configuration drift.
antipattern.fix: Push fix through new template version; treat instances as cattle.
